methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.__init__
documentation:
---
param asset_hash: 'Specify a custom hash for this asset. If `assetHashType` is set
  it must be set to `AssetHashType.CUSTOM`. For consistency, this custom hash will
  be SHA256 hashed and encoded as hex. The resulting hash will be the asset hash.
  NOTE: the hash is used in order to identify a specific revision of the asset, and
  used for optimizing and caching deployment activities related to this asset such
  as packaging, uploading to Amazon S3, etc. If you chose to customize the hash, you
  will need to make sure it is updated every time the asset changes, or otherwise
  it is possible that some deployments will not be invalidated. Default: - based on
  `assetHashType`'
param asset_hash_type: 'Specifies the type of hash to calculate for this asset. If
  `assetHash` is configured, this option must be `undefined` or `AssetHashType.CUSTOM`.
  Default: - the default is `AssetHashType.SOURCE`, but if `assetHash` is explicitly
  specified this value defaults to `AssetHashType.CUSTOM`.'
param bundling: 'Bundle the asset by executing a command in a Docker container or
  a custom bundling provider. The asset path will be mounted at `/asset-input`. The
  Docker container is responsible for putting content at `/asset-output`. The content
  at `/asset-output` will be zipped and used as the final asset. Default: - uploaded
  as-is to S3 if the asset is a regular file or a .zip file, archived into a .zip
  file and uploaded to S3 otherwise'
param commands: 'Shell commands executed right after the asset zip is extracted to
  the build environment. Default: No command is executed.'
param exclude: 'File paths matching the patterns will be excluded. See `ignoreMode`
  to set the matching behavior. Has no effect on Assets bundled using the `bundling`
  property. Default: - nothing is excluded'
param extract_path: 'Relative path from a build directory to the directory where the
  asset is extracted. Default: basename of the asset path.'
param follow_symlinks: 'A strategy for how to handle symlinks. Default: SymlinkFollowMode.NEVER'
param ignore_mode: 'The ignore behavior to use for `exclude` patterns. Default: IgnoreMode.GLOB'
param path: 'The disk location of the asset. The path should refer to one of the following:
  - A regular file or a .zip file, in which case the file will be uploaded as-is to
  S3. - A directory, in which case it will be archived into a .zip file and uploaded
  to S3.'
param readers: 'A list of principals that should be able to read this asset from S3.
  You can use `asset.grantRead(principal)` to grant read permissions later. Default:
  - No principals that can read file asset.'
---

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.asset_hash
documentation:
Specify a custom hash for this asset.

If `assetHashType` is set it must
be set to `AssetHashType.CUSTOM`. For consistency, this custom hash will
be SHA256 hashed and encoded as hex. The resulting hash will be the asset
hash.

NOTE: the hash is used in order to identify a specific revision of the asset, and
used for optimizing and caching deployment activities related to this asset such as
packaging, uploading to Amazon S3, etc. If you chose to customize the hash, you will
need to make sure it is updated every time the asset changes, or otherwise it is
possible that some deployments will not be invalidated.

```
default

based on assetHashType
```

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.asset_hash_type
documentation:
Specifies the type of hash to calculate for this asset.

If `assetHash` is configured, this option must be `undefined` or
`AssetHashType.CUSTOM`.

```
default

```

- the default is `AssetHashType.SOURCE`, but if `assetHash` is

explicitly specified this value defaults to `AssetHashType.CUSTOM`.

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.bundling
documentation:
Bundle the asset by executing a command in a Docker container or a custom bundling provider.

The asset path will be mounted at `/asset-input`. The Docker
container is responsible for putting content at `/asset-output`.
The content at `/asset-output` will be zipped and used as the
final asset.

```
default

```

- uploaded as-is to S3 if the asset is a regular file or a .zip file,

archived into a .zip file and uploaded to S3 otherwise

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.exclude
documentation:
File paths matching the patterns will be excluded.

See `ignoreMode` to set the matching behavior.
Has no effect on Assets bundled using the `bundling` property.

```
default

nothing is excluded
```

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.follow_symlinks
documentation:
A strategy for how to handle symlinks.

:default: SymlinkFollowMode.NEVER

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.ignore_mode
documentation:
The ignore behavior to use for `exclude` patterns.

```
default

IgnoreMode.GLOB
```

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.readers
documentation:
A list of principals that should be able to read this asset from S3.

You can use `asset.grantRead(principal)` to grant read permissions later.

```
default

No principals that can read file asset.
```

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.path
documentation:
The disk location of the asset.

The path should refer to one of the following:

- A regular file or a .zip file, in which case the file will be uploaded as-is to S3.
- A directory, in which case it will be archived into a .zip file and uploaded to S3.

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.commands
documentation:
Shell commands executed right after the asset zip is extracted to the build environment.

:default: No command is executed.

methods.name: deploy_time_build.AssetConfig.extract_path
documentation:
Relative path from a build directory to the directory where the asset is extracted.

:default: basename of the asset path.

classes.name: deploy_time_build.ContainerImageBuild

methods.name: deploy_time_build.ContainerImageBuild.__init__
documentation:
---
param build_args: 'Build args to pass to the `docker build` command. Since Docker
  build arguments are resolved before deployment, keys and values cannot refer to
  unresolved tokens (such as `lambda.functionArn` or `queue.queueUrl`). Default: -
  no build args are passed'
param directory: The directory where the Dockerfile is stored. Any directory inside
  with a name that matches the CDK output folder (cdk.out by default) will be excluded
  from the asset
param exclude: 'File paths matching the patterns will be excluded. See `ignoreMode`
  to set the matching behavior. Has no effect on Assets bundled using the `bundling`
  property. Default: - nothing is excluded'
param extra_hash: 'Extra information to encode into the fingerprint (e.g. build instructions
  and other inputs). Default: - hash is only based on source content'
param file: 'Path to the Dockerfile (relative to the directory). Default: ''Dockerfile'''
param follow_symlinks: 'A strategy for how to handle symlinks. Default: SymlinkFollowMode.NEVER'
param id: '-'
param ignore_mode: 'The ignore behavior to use for `exclude` patterns. Default: IgnoreMode.GLOB'
param invalidation: 'Options to control which parameters are used to invalidate the
  asset hash. Default: - hash all parameters'
param network_mode: 'Networking mode for the RUN commands during build. Support docker
  API 1.25+. Default: - no networking mode specified (the default networking mode
  `NetworkMode.DEFAULT` will be used)'
param platform: 'Platform to build for. *Requires Docker Buildx*. Default: - no platform
  specified (the current machine architecture will be used)'
param repository: 'The ECR repository to push the image. Default: create a new ECR
  repository'
param scope: '-'
param tag: 'The tag when to push the image. Default: use assetHash as tag'
param target: 'Docker target to build to. Default: - no target'
param zstd_compression: 'Use zstd for compressing a container image. Default: false'
---

methods.name: deploy_time_build.ContainerImageBuild.to_ecs_docker_image_code
documentation: Get the instance of {@link ContainerImage} for an ECS task definition.

methods.name: deploy_time_build.ContainerImageBuild.to_lambda_docker_image_code

Build a container image and push it to an ECR repository on deploy-time.

classes.name: deploy_time_build.NodejsBuild

methods.name: deploy_time_build.NodejsBuild.__init__
documentation:
---
param assets: The AssetProps from which s3-assets are created and copied to the build
  environment.
param build_commands: 'Shell commands to build your project. They are executed on
  the working directory you specified. Default: [''npm run build'']'
param build_environment: 'Environment variables injected to the build environment.
  You can use CDK deploy-time values as well as literals. Default: {}'
param destination_bucket: S3 Bucket to which your build artifacts are finally deployed.
param destination_key_prefix: 'Key prefix to deploy your build artifact. Default:
  ''/'''
param distribution: 'The distribution you are using to publish you build artifact.
  If any specified, the caches are invalidated on new artifact deployments. Default:
  No distribution'
param exclude_common_files: 'If true, common unnecessary files/directories such as
  .DS_Store, .git, node_modules, etc are excluded from the assets by default. Default:
  true'
param id: '-'
param nodejs_version: 'The version of Node.js to use in a build environment. Available
  versions: 12, 14, 16, 18, 20. Default: 18'
param output_env_file: 'If true, a .env file is uploaded to an S3 bucket with values
  of `buildEnvironment` property. You can copy it to your local machine by running
  the command in the stack output. Default: false'
param output_source_directory: Relative path from the working directory to the directory
  where the build artifacts are output.
param scope: '-'
param working_directory: 'Relative path from the build directory to the directory
  where build commands run. Default: assetProps[0].extractPath'
---

methods.name: deploy_time_build.NodejsBuild.grant_principal
documentation: The principal to grant permissions to.
Build Node.js app and optionally publish the artifact to an S3 bucket.

If any specified, the caches are invalidated on new artifact deployments.

If true, common unnecessary files/directories such as .DS_Store, .git, node_modules, etc are excluded from the assets by default.

methods.name: deploy_time_build.NodejsBuildProps.output_env_file

If true, a .env file is uploaded to an S3 bucket with values of `buildEnvironment` property. You can copy it to your local machine by running the command in the stack output.

```

false
```

Relative path from the build directory to the directory where build commands run.

:default: assetProps[0].extractPath

classes.name: deploy_time_build.SociIndexBuild

methods.name: deploy_time_build.SociIndexBuild.__init__
documentation:
---
param id: '-'
param image_tag: The tag of the container image you want to build index for.
param repository: The ECR repository your container image is stored. You can only
  specify a repository in the same environment (account/region). The index artifact
  will be uploaded to this repository.
param scope: '-'
---

methods.name: deploy_time_build.SociIndexBuild.from_docker_image_asset
documentation:
A utility method to create a SociIndexBuild construct from a DockerImageAsset instance.

```
param scope



param id



param image_asset

```

Build and publish a SOCI index for a container image.

A SOCI index helps start Fargate tasks faster in some cases.
Please read the following document for more details: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/userguide/container-considerations.html

methods.name: deploy_time_build.SociIndexBuildProps.__init__
documentation:
---
param image_tag: The tag of the container image you want to build index for.
param repository: The ECR repository your container image is stored. You can only
  specify a repository in the same environment (account/region). The index artifact
  will be uploaded to this repository.
---

methods.name: deploy_time_build.SociIndexBuildProps.image_tag
documentation: The tag of the container image you want to build index for.

methods.name: deploy_time_build.SociIndexBuildProps.repository
documentation:
The ECR repository your container image is stored.

You can only specify a repository in the same environment (account/region).
The index artifact will be uploaded to this repository.
